behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at module handoff boundaries (manifest validation -> loader execution path). A unit test of the validator alone can miss a bug where the loader still imports/executes plugin code. Rejected higher levels: no real HTTP/subprocess/external API is required, and no AI/ML output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with an invalid manifest and an entry module that would leave a detectable in-process side effect if imported. Run the real loader wiring against that directory and assert: (1) plugin is rejected with validation error, (2) side effect never occurs, (3) other valid plugins still proceed as expected.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration across modules (dependency graph resolution + hook invocation ordering). Unit tests of sort logic alone can miss wiring/order bugs in loader execution. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only requires local plugin loading and hook order, not subprocess/network/AI.
behaviors.test_description: Set up two real test plugins in a temp directory where A depends on B. Record hook invocation order in shared test state. Run the loader and assert B.onLoad executes before A.onLoad.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection must be validated in full loader flow: graph construction, error reporting, and suppression of loads. Unit-only graph tests can miss that loader still executes plugins. Rejected higher levels: no real external infra or model behavior is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create two plugins with mutual dependencies. Run loader, assert cycle error/report includes both plugins, and assert neither plugin module/hook executes (no side effects, no onLoad calls).

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is a sequencing guarantee across loader internals and Node module import execution. A unit test of call order in one function can miss real import/execution timing interactions. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not require subprocess/network/external API/AI.
behaviors.test_description: Use a plugin entry module that sets a module-level flag at top level during import, and onLoad asserts/records that flag is already set. Run loader and assert import-side effect happened before onLoad invocation.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on interaction between hook execution, failure handling, dependency filtering, and continuation logic across multiple plugins. Unit tests on error handler alone can miss stop-the-world regressions. Rejected higher levels: no requirement for real network/subprocess or AI model output.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins where X throws in onLoad, Y is independent, and Z depends on X. Run loader and assert: X failure is reported, Y still loads, Z does not load, and loader completes remaining initialization.
